reasoning: The US Treasury yield curve is currently upward sloping (normal) and steepening (rising trend), which signifies market expectations of future economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4, 98b8f9c4befb]. This is supported by the 10-year real interest rate being low relative to history (32nd percentile), which increases liquidity and reduces volatility in risk assets [99ad6cdde172]. While the VIX remains in an 'elevated' regime, its recent 3-month and 6-month trends are falling, aligning with the bullish signals from the yield curve and real rates.
